reasoning: The investigative process began by identifying candidates via the ranked list, which highlighted three KPIs on the E2 interface. Although e2_du_throughput_dl_kbps has the lowest distance (Rank 1), I applied the Metric Severity Prioritization heuristic. Volumetric changes (throughput) are often symptoms of underlying hard errors. e2_du_packet_loss_ul_percentage (Rank 2) is a severe degradation metric (packet loss) and therefore carries more diagnostic weight as a potential root cause. Graph queries confirmed that the E2 interface is affected and that this failure is coupled with anomalies across all other interfaces (F1-c, F1-u, and A1) and all O-RAN components. The fact that the highest-ranking anomalies are concentrated on the E2 interface indicates it as the origin point, with subsequent failures on F1 and A1 being downstream symptoms of a disrupted control/management plane between the O-DU and the Near-RT RIC.
actionable_steps: 1. Inspect the physical and logical transport layer of the E2 interface for connectivity issues or congestion. 2. Verify the health and configuration of the E2 Agent on the O-DU and the Near-RT RIC. 3. Review recent configuration changes to the Near-RT RIC's xApps that may be causing instability in the E2 signaling.
